questions.text: A small block of mass $m_1$ is released from rest at the top of a curve-shaped, frictionless wedge of mass $m_2$ which sits on a frictionless horizontal surface as shown. When the block leaves the wedge its velocity is measured to be 4.00 m/s to the right as shown in the figure. If the mass of the block is doubled to become 2$m_1$, what can be said about the speed with which it leaves the wedge?

choices.a: Its speed is less than 4.00 m/s
choices.b: Its speed is equal to 4.00 m/s
choices.c: Its speed is greater than 4.00 m/s
choices.d: Not enough information is given.

questions.diagram_description: Two side-by-side diagrams showing (a) a small block $m_1$ at the top of a curved wedge $m_2$ on a horizontal surface, and (b) the block moving to the right at 4.00 m/s after leaving the wedge, with the wedge also moving.

questions.text: Consider the electric charges A, B, C shown in the figure below, where $q$ is a positive number. Which answer correctly describes the magnitude of the net force experienced by the charges?

choices.c: $F_B > F_A > F_C$
choices.d: $F_A = F_B = F_C$

questions.diagram_description: Three charges arranged in a line: charge A with value $-q$, charge B with value $+q$ separated by distance $d$ from A, and charge C with value $+q$ separated by distance $d$ from B.

questions.text: A long, straight wire carries a current that decreases linearly with time. What is the direction of the induced electric field outside the wire?

choices.a: Parallel to the current in the wire, in the same direction.
choices.b: Parallel to the current in the wire, in the opposite direction.
choices.c: Pointing radially outward from the wire.
choices.d: Pointing radially inward toward the wire.
choices.e: There is no induced electric field outside the wire.

questions.text: A car driven at a constant speed turns left. Which force makes the car turn?

choices.a: The force of friction, directed towards the left.
choices.b: The force of friction, directed towards the right.
choices.c: The force with which the driver is turning the steering wheel, directed towards the left.
choices.d: The force with which the driver is turning the steering wheel, directed towards the right.

questions.text: A cyclist stops pedaling at a velocity $v = 36$ km/h and notices that the bike has been moving for $d = 500$ m before it stops. The total mass of the bike, the biker and her camping equipment is $m = 100$ kg. What is the average combined force on the bicycle due to friction and drag?
A child is swinging to and fro on a playground swing. At the instant the chains of the swing are vertical, what is the direction of the child's acceleration?

choices.c: In the direction of motion.
choices.d: Opposite to the direction of motion.
choices.e: At that moment, the acceleration is zero.

questions.text: A bullet of mass 5 g is accelerated in a rifle barrel with an approximately constant force of 2500 N. The mass of the rifle is 5 kg. What is the force pushing the rifle back?

choices.d: 0 N

questions.text: Two balls of different masses collide head-on. After the collision, the balls remain at rest, and no external forces act on them. Which statement is true about the speeds of the balls before the collision?

choices.a: They must have been different, and the collision was inelastic.
choices.b: They must have been different, and the collision was elastic.
choices.c: They must have been identical, and the collision was inelastic.
choices.d: They must have been identical, and the collision was elastic.
choices.e: No combination of speed values could have caused both balls to stop after the collision.

questions.text: A Ferris wheel is turning about a horizontal axis through its center. The linear speed of a passenger sitting on the rim is constant. Which one of the following sentences about the passenger's acceleration is correct?

choices.a: Its magnitude at the highest point is higher than at the lowest point, and it is downwards at both points.
choices.b: Both its magnitude and direction are the same at the highest and at the lowest points.
choices.c: Its magnitude is the same at the highest and at the lowest points, but the directions are opposite.
choices.d: Its magnitude at the highest point is smaller than at the lowest point, and it is downwards at both points.

questions.text: A uniform rod is suspended on two thin strings as shown below. Which string has a larger tension force?

choices.c: The tension force is the same on both
choices.d: It depends on the angle

questions.diagram_description: A diagram showing a uniform rod suspended by two strings, with one string vertical and one string at an angle

questions.text: Two point charges $-2Q$ and $+Q$ are placed on the $x$-axis with $-2Q$ at $x = 0$ and $+Q$ at $x = a$. Which of the following statements is true?

choices.a: There is no point on the $x$-axis where the electric field is zero.
choices.b: There is a point on the $x$-axis, at $x < 0$, where the electric field is zero.
choices.c: There is a point on the $x$-axis, between the charges $(0 < x < a)$, where the electric field is zero.
choices.d: There is a point on the $x$-axis, at $x > a$, where the electric field is zero.

questions.text: In a lab experiment, a laser beam hits a semicircular glass object off the centre axis. The ray enters perpendicularly to the surface, and the environment is air. Which ray diagram is correct?

choices.c: Option C
choices.d: Option D

questions.diagram_description: Six semicircular glass objects labeled A through F showing different laser beam paths entering and exiting the curved surfaces

questions.text: You put two identical ice cubes on plates of different materials. One cube is put on an aluminium plate, and the other on a glass plate. Both plates have been in the room for a long time prior to the experiment. You notice that the ice melts faster on the metal plate. Why?

choices.a: The ice is in thermal equilibrium with the plastic plate, but not with the metal plate.
choices.b: The metal plate conducts heat to the ice more rapidly than the plastic plate.
choices.c: The metal plate holds more heat than the plastic plate.
choices.d: The metal plate was warmer than the plastic plate initially.

questions.text: Two black objects of the same diameter, a sphere and a flat disk, are placed in a parallel beam of light. The plane of the disk is perpendicular to the beam. What can be said about the force acting on them?

choices.b: The force is larger on the disk than on the sphere.
choices.c: The force is larger on the sphere than on the disk.
choices.d: The force is the same on both objects.

questions.text: Adrianne has a device that emits photons with a wavelength of λ = 1.498 km. Kwan has a similar device that emits photons at a frequency of f = 201 kHz. Whose device emits photons in air at the highest frequency?

choices.c: The frequencies are the same.
choices.d: There is not enough information to compare.

questions.text: A girl sitting on a floating mattress in a small pool holds a model of a motorboat in her hands. When she puts it in the water to start driving it with her remote control, what happens to the water level in the pool?

choices.a: It rises by a small amount.
choices.b: It drops by a small amount.
choices.c: It stays the same.
choices.d: The qualitative result depends on the weight of the model boat.

questions.text: Which of the accompanying P–V diagrams best represents an isothermal process, that is a process occurring at constant temperature?

choices.c: Option c
choices.d: Option d

questions.diagram_description: Five P-V diagrams labeled a through e showing different relationships: a) horizontal line, b) curved decreasing line, c) diagonal increasing line, d) curved increasing line, e) vertical line

questions.text: Two identical objects of mass m are connected to a massless string which is hung over two frictionless pulleys as shown below. If everything is at rest, what is the tension in the cord?

choices.c: More than mg, but less than 2mg.
choices.d: Exactly 2mg.

questions.diagram_description: Two masses m connected by a string over two pulleys, with the masses hanging on either side

questions.text: Car A and car B are both traveling down a straight highway at 90 km/h. Car A is only 6.0 metres behind car B. The driver of car B brakes, slowing down with a constant deceleration of 2.0 m/s². After 1.2 seconds, the driver of car A begins to brake, also at 2.0 m/s², but this is insufficient and the cars eventually collide, both still moving forward. What is the relative velocity of the two cars at the moment of the collision?

choices.d: 21 m/s

questions.text: Two interfering waves have the same wavelength, frequency, and amplitude. They are traveling in the same direction but are 90 degrees out of phase. Compared to the individual waves, what can be said about the resultant wave?

choices.a: It will have the same amplitude and velocity, but a different wavelength.
choices.b: It will have the same amplitude and wavelength, but a different velocity.
choices.c: It will have the same wavelength and velocity, but a different amplitude.
choices.d: It will have the same amplitude and frequency, but a different velocity.
choices.e: It will have the same frequency and velocity, but a different wavelength.

questions.text: A spinning ice skater has an initial kinetic energy $\frac{1}{2}I\omega^2$. She pulls in her outstretched arms, decreasing her moment of inertia to $\frac{1}{4}I$. What is her new angular speed?

choices.c: $\omega$
choices.d: $2\omega$

questions.text: A ball of mass $m$ is thrown vertically upward. Instead of neglecting air resistance, assume that the force of air resistance has a magnitude proportional to the ball's velocity, but pointing in the opposite direction. What is the ball's acceleration at the highest point?

choices.d: Greater than $g$.

questions.text: A hypothetical planet has density $\rho$, radius $R$, and surface gravitational acceleration $g$. What would be the acceleration due to gravity at the surface of a planet with double the radius, and the same planetary density?

You are given two lenses placed close together: a converging lens with focal length +10 cm, and a diverging lens with focal length −20 cm. Which of the following would produce a virtual image that is larger than the object?

choices.a: Placing the object 5 cm from the converging lens.
choices.b: Placing the object 15 cm from the converging lens.
choices.c: Placing the object 25 cm from the converging lens.
choices.d: Placing the object 15 cm from the diverging lens.
choices.e: Placing the object 25 cm from the diverging lens.

questions.text: An electromagnetic wave is propagating in vacuum in the positive $s$ direction. At an instant when its magnetic field $\vec{B}$ at a certain position is in the positive $x$ direction and getting stronger, what happens to its electric field $\vec{E}$ at that same position?

choices.a: $\vec{E}$ is in the positive $x$ direction and getting weaker.
choices.b: $\vec{E}$ is in the negative $x$ direction and getting stronger.
choices.c: $\vec{E}$ is in the positive $y$ direction and getting weaker.
choices.d: $\vec{E}$ is in the negative $y$ direction and getting stronger.
